---
title: "Week 13: Latent Response & GLM"
date: "2026-11-09"
---

## Overview

This week introduces a deeper theoretical perspective on the models you have been fitting. The latent variable formulation reinterprets binary and ordinal outcomes as observable manifestations of a continuous, unobserved response. Building on this idea, the Generalized Linear Model (GLM) framework unifies the regression models covered throughout the course by specifying three components: a random component (the outcome distribution), a systematic component (the linear predictor), and a link function that connects them. You will see how OLS, logistic regression, Poisson regression, and other models are all special cases of the GLM.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Describe the latent variable formulation underlying binary regression models
- Explain how a threshold applied to a continuous latent response produces an observed binary outcome
- Identify the three components of a Generalized Linear Model (random, systematic, link)
- Compare the logit and probit link functions in terms of their assumptions and interpretations
- Explain how the GLM framework unifies the regression models studied in this course

## Readings

- {{< fa book-open >}} **Required**: [Chapter 12: Latent Response & Generalized Linear Models]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/12-latent-response-glm.html){target="_blank"}

## R Lab

- {{< fa laptop-code >}} [Lab 12: Advanced Logistic Regression]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}/labs/lab12-logistic-advanced.html){target="_blank"}

## Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Latent variable** | An unobserved continuous variable that underlies an observed categorical outcome |
| **Threshold model** | A model in which the observed category is determined by whether the latent variable exceeds a threshold |
| **Generalized Linear Model (GLM)** | A flexible framework that extends linear regression by specifying a random component, systematic component, and link function |
| **Random component** | The probability distribution assumed for the outcome variable (e.g., normal, binomial, Poisson) |
| **Systematic component** | The linear predictor formed by the regression coefficients and explanatory variables |
| **Link function** | The function that connects the expected value of the outcome to the linear predictor |
| **Logit link** | The link function based on the logistic distribution, mapping probabilities to log-odds |
| **Probit link** | The link function based on the normal distribution, mapping probabilities to z-scores |
